what_it_does: A JSONL ledger where each line records a code or design substrate with a one-line purpose, a real/partial/stub status, a list of implemented features, connected systems, notable caveats, and a relative file path.
observations: JSONL format with one substrate record per line
Each record uses the fields what, real_or_stub, implements, connects_to, notable, and rel
Contains a large cluster of math-verification scripts across multiple round directories
Includes AI provider implementations, stubs, and partial adapters under src/ai
Includes content-workbench scripts, SQL schema, and configuration files
Many notable fields flag 'no external API calls', 'pure logic', 'no TODOs', or 'stub'
File content is truncated before the end of the listed substrates
No code symbols are present in the file itself
standouts: Heavy concentration of math-verification agent scripts with adversarial/counter-claim round structure
Mix of real-working, partial, and stub AI provider modules
Truncated tail cuts off remaining entries
conventions: Consistent per-line schema across all records
Uses real_or_stub as a coarse readiness label
rel paths are relative to content-workbench/
Notable field carries caveats and context
concerns: Content is truncated, so the ledger is incomplete in this artifact
real_or_stub labels appear subjective and not uniformly applied
Some 'real-working' entries are described as print-only or hardcoded simulations
No line numbers, timestamps, or authorship metadata
notes: A truncated, schema-consistent inventory of content-workbench substrates, dominated by math-verification scripts and AI provider code.
questions: What process generated these substrate summaries?
How is real_or_stub determined and validated?
What is the intended consumer of this ledger?
How much of the file is missing after truncation?
Are duplicate rel paths across vault rounds intentional separate records?
apparent_themes: AI provider abstraction and capability tracking
intention_signals: Build a machine-readable, annotated index of project files to support migration scoping, audit, or fusion of code and design substrates.
novelty: The ledger treats math-verification scripts as design substrates and captures adversarial round relationships, not just imports or AST symbols.
contribution: A structured, queryable map of files with readiness status, claimed capabilities, dependencies, and caveats, useful for identifying stubs, partial work, and verification coverage.
summary_for_embedding: A truncated JSONL ledger listing content-workbench code and design substrates with fields for purpose, real/partial/stub status, implemented features, connections, notable caveats, and relative paths.
intention_for_embedding: Produced to catalog and classify project substrates for migration, audit, or fusion analysis.
